gloss.en: a spoken or written comment expressing an opinion

words.example: She made a rude remark about my work.

gloss.en: unusual or surprising enough to be noticed

words.example: It was a remarkable achievement for someone so young.

gloss.en: in a surprising way that people notice

words.example: The car is in remarkably good condition for its age.

gloss.en: to arrange or organize something in a new way

words.example: We need to reorganise the files by date.

gloss.en: to arrange or organize something in a new way

words.example: The boss decided to reorganize the whole team.

gloss.en: to paint something again

words.example: They plan to repaint the old house this summer.

gloss.en: all the works a performer or group can do

words.example: The band has a wide repertoire of songs.

gloss.en: the act of doing or saying something again

words.example: Learning a language needs a lot of repetition.

gloss.en: an exact copy of something

words.example: The museum sells a small replica of the statue.

gloss.en: the writing about and presenting of news

words.example: The newspaper is known for its accurate reporting.

gloss.en: a person chosen to act or speak for others

words.example: A sales representative will visit your office tomorrow.

gloss.en: to control or hold back a feeling or action

words.example: She tried to repress her anger during the meeting.

gloss.en: the act of controlling people or feelings by force

words.example: The people suffered years of political repression.

gloss.en: a new printing of a book already published

words.example: This book is a reprint of the 1990 edition.

gloss.en: to tell someone you disapprove of what they did

words.example: The teacher began to reprove the noisy students.

gloss.en: the opinion people have about someone or something

words.example: The hotel has a good reputation for service.

gloss.en: something that you need or must have

words.example: A degree is a requirement for this job.

gloss.en: the act of saving someone from danger

words.example: The rescue of the lost hikers took all night.

gloss.en: a supply kept for future use when needed

words.example: The country has a large reserve of oil.

gloss.en: living in a particular place

words.example: The resident population grows every summer.

gloss.en: to officially leave your job or position

words.example: She was forced to resign due to ill health.

gloss.en: to refuse to accept and try to stop something

words.example: They are determined to resist any pressure to change.

gloss.en: a firm decision to do or not do something

words.example: My New Year's resolution is to exercise more.

gloss.en: to find an acceptable solution to a problem

words.example: They met to resolve the dispute peacefully.

gloss.en: deep, clear and continuing in sound

words.example: He has a deep, resonant voice.

gloss.en: a place where many people go on holiday

words.example: We spent a week at a seaside resort.

gloss.en: in a way people accept as proper or good

words.example: He was respectably dressed for the interview.

gloss.en: admired by many people for good qualities

words.example: She is a highly respected doctor in this town.

gloss.en: the process of breathing

words.example: The nurse checked the patient's respiration.

gloss.en: relating to breathing or the breathing organs

words.example: Smoking can cause serious respiratory problems.

gloss.en: to bring back a former situation or feeling

words.example: The measures should restore public confidence.

gloss.en: to limit the size, amount or range of something

words.example: Speed is restricted to 30 mph in towns.

gloss.en: a rule that limits what you can do

words.example: There are travel restrictions in some areas.

gloss.en: the selling of goods to the public in shops

words.example: The recommended retail price is ten dollars.

gloss.en: a person or business that sells goods to the public

words.example: This online retailer offers free delivery.

gloss.en: to keep something; to continue to have something

words.example: The town wants to retain its old buildings.

gloss.en: to make something slower or later

words.example: Cold weather can retard the growth of plants.

gloss.en: the light-sensitive layer at the back of the eye

words.example: The doctor examined the retina of her eye.

gloss.en: the act of stopping work, usually due to age

words.example: At 60, he was now approaching retirement.

gloss.en: to make something known to someone

words.example: He refused to reveal the secret.

gloss.en: money received from taxes or a business

words.example: The government faces a shortfall in tax revenue.

gloss.en: a feeling of deep respect or admiration

words.example: They treat the old teacher with great reverence.

gloss.en: a change or set of changes to something

words.example: He made some minor revisions to the report.

gloss.en: an attempt by many people to change the government

words.example: The revolution changed the whole country.

gloss.en: to change something completely and for the better

words.example: The internet helped revolutionise how we work.

gloss.en: to change something completely and for the better

words.example: New tools can revolutionize the whole industry.

gloss.en: to move in a circle around a central point

words.example: The planets revolve around the sun.

gloss.en: something given for good work or behaviour

words.example: They offered a financial reward for the lost dog.

gloss.en: a large heavy animal with horns on its nose

words.example: We saw a rhinoceros at the zoo.

gloss.en: to have the same end sound as another word

words.example: The word 'cat' rhymes with 'hat'.

gloss.en: a strong regular pattern of sounds or movements

words.example: They danced to the rhythm of the music.

gloss.en: having a strong regular pattern of beats

words.example: We heard the rhythmic sound of the drums.

gloss.en: one of the curved bones around the chest

words.example: He broke a rib in the accident.

gloss.en: to make free of something unwanted

words.example: She wanted to be rid of all her old clothes.

gloss.en: a question that is a clever puzzle to solve

words.example: The children tried to solve the riddle.

gloss.en: a person who rides a horse, bike or motorcycle

words.example: The rider fell off her horse.

gloss.en: a long narrow area along the top of a hill

words.example: We walked along the mountain ridge.

gloss.en: very silly or unreasonable

words.example: I look ridiculous in this hat.

gloss.en: the quality of being morally good or right

words.example: He spoke with a strong sense of righteousness.

gloss.en: for a good reason; correctly

words.example: She was rightly angry about the mistake.

gloss.en: the outer edge of a round object

words.example: There was a chip on the rim of the cup.

gloss.en: involving the chance of something bad happening

words.example: Starting a business can be a risky decision.

gloss.en: a formal ceremony, often religious

words.example: The wedding followed an ancient rite.

gloss.en: a person or group competing with another

words.example: The two teams are old rivals.

gloss.en: to walk or travel around with no fixed plan

words.example: The sheep roam freely across the hills.

gloss.en: to make a long, loud, deep sound

words.example: The lion began to roar loudly.

gloss.en: cooked in an oven or over a fire

words.example: We had roast chicken for dinner.

gloss.en: to steal money or property from a person or place

words.example: The men tried to rob a bank.

gloss.en: the crime of stealing money or goods

words.example: The bank robbery shocked the whole town.

gloss.en: a tube-shaped vehicle driven into space by gases

words.example: The space rocket was launched at dawn.

gloss.en: full of rocks or made of rock

words.example: We climbed up the steep rocky path.

gloss.en: an exciting love relationship between two people

words.example: They had a holiday romance last summer.

gloss.en: a person you share a room or home with

words.example: My roommate and I cook dinner together.

gloss.en: the part of a plant that grows underground

words.example: The tree has deep spreading roots.

gloss.en: a sweet-smelling flower that grows on a thorny bush

words.example: He gave her a bunch of red roses.

gloss.en: pink and healthy; hopeful and positive

words.example: The future looks rosy for the company.

gloss.en: approximately but not exactly

words.example: Sales are up by roughly 10 per cent.

gloss.en: having a shape like a circle or a ball

words.example: The food was served on a round plate.

gloss.en: a way you follow to get from one place to another

words.example: Which is the best route to take?

gloss.en: a fixed way of doing things regularly

words.example: Getting up early is part of my daily routine.

gloss.en: members of a royal family

words.example: The hotel often hosts royalty and stars.

gloss.en: to move your hand or a cloth over a surface

words.example: He sat up and began to rub his eyes.

gloss.en: a strong elastic material used to make many things

words.example: The children played with a rubber ball.

gloss.en: broken stones and bricks from a ruined building

words.example: After the storm, the house was just rubble.

gloss.en: a thick mat that covers part of a floor

words.example: There is a soft rug beside the bed.

gloss.en: the state of being destroyed or badly damaged

words.example: Many old churches fell into ruin.

gloss.en: causing great damage or financial loss

words.example: The war had a ruinous effect on the economy.

gloss.en: connected with or like the countryside

words.example: They moved to a quiet rural area.

gloss.en: a sudden strong movement or action

words.example: Shoppers made a rush for the exits.

gloss.en: covered with rust; out of practice

words.example: The old gate was covered in rusty metal.

gloss.en: to sink or bend down in the middle

words.example: The old shelf began to sag under the books.

gloss.en: a very wise person, especially an old one

words.example: People came to the old sage for advice.

gloss.en: strong cloth that catches wind to move a boat

words.example: The wind filled the boat's sail.

gloss.en: money paid to an employee, usually each month

words.example: She earns an annual salary of forty thousand.

gloss.en: skill in selling things to people

words.example: His clever salesmanship helped the shop grow.

gloss.en: a person whose job is selling things

words.example: A friendly salesperson helped me choose a phone.

gloss.en: a woman whose job is selling things

words.example: The saleswoman showed us several new cars.

gloss.en: a shop for hair, beauty or similar services

words.example: She got her hair cut at the salon.

gloss.en: a lively Latin American dance or music

words.example: They learned to dance the salsa together.

gloss.en: containing or tasting of salt

words.example: The soup was too salty to eat.

gloss.en: a small part used to show what a group is like

words.example: They tested a random sample of students.

gloss.en: a soft rock formed from grains of sand

words.example: The old church was built from sandstone.

gloss.en: connected with clean and healthy conditions

words.example: The kitchen must be kept in sanitary condition.

gloss.en: systems for keeping places clean and healthy

words.example: Clean water and good sanitation save lives.

gloss.en: a device sent into space to orbit a planet

words.example: The weather satellite sends images to Earth.

gloss.en: the good feeling you get from achieving something

words.example: She gets great satisfaction from her work.

gloss.en: good enough for a particular purpose

words.example: His work was satisfactory but not excellent.

gloss.en: pleased because you got what you wanted

words.example: A satisfied customer often comes back.

gloss.en: to please someone by giving what they want

words.example: Nothing seems to satisfy him these days.

gloss.en: an amount of time or money you do not spend

words.example: Buy three and make a saving of one dollar.

gloss.en: money you have kept, usually in a bank

words.example: They used their savings to buy a house.

gloss.en: the size or extent of something

words.example: They entertain guests on a large scale.

gloss.en: shocking behaviour that people think is wrong

words.example: The political scandal was in all the papers.

gloss.en: a mark left on the skin after a wound heals

words.example: He has a small scar on his cheek.

gloss.en: almost not; only just

words.example: I could scarcely hear her quiet voice.

gloss.en: a situation that makes many people afraid

words.example: There was a health scare about the water.

gloss.en: a bright red colour

words.example: Her cheeks turned scarlet with shame.

gloss.en: a description of how things might happen

words.example: Let me suggest a possible scenario.

gloss.en: a pleasant smell

words.example: The garden was full of the scent of roses.

gloss.en: a plan listing tasks and when to do them

words.example: I have a hectic schedule for the next few days.
